item.title: minicaml, a didactical OCaml-like functional programming language.
item.description: minicaml is a small, purely functional interpreted programming language with a didactical purpose. I wrote minicaml for the Programming 2 course at the University of Pisa, taught by Professors Gianluigi Ferrari and Francesca Levi. It is based on the teachers’ minicaml, an evaluation example to show how interpreters work. It is an interpreted subset of Caml, with eager evaluation and only local (let-in) declaration statements. I have added a simple parser and lexer made with menhir and ocamllex (learn more).
